name: Sarween Tools
text:
When 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION, reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1.

During your turn, when a space dock in your home system uses PRODUCTION, you may exhaust this card to reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1 for each of your space docks on a planet in home systems.

name: Integrated Economy
text:
When you use the PRODUCTION abilities in your home system, for every 2 of your structures on the game board, you may place 1 of the produced units in adjacent systems that contain your units.

ACTION: Exhaust this card and spend 1 command token from your strategy pool to use the PRODUCTION abilities of your units in 1 system. If that system is your home system, gain 1 command token.

name: X-89 Bacterial Weapon
text:
After you destroy 1 or more of another player's ground forces on a planet during a tactical action, you may exhaust this card to destroy all of that player's remaining ground forces on that planet.

When 1 or more of your ground forces on a planet would be destroyed, you may exhaust this card; half of those ground forces, rounded up, are not destroyed.

name: Neural Motivator
text: The maximum number of action cards you can have in your hand is increased by 2. During the status phase, draw 2 action cards instead of 1.

name: Self-Assembly Routines
text:
After 1 of your units is destroyed, you may exhaust this card to capture that unit.

At the end of a combat round, you may return 1 captured unit to produce a unit of the same type and place it with your units participating in the combat. This effect may only be used once per combat.

name: Predictive Intelligence
text:
At the end of your turn, you may exhaust this card to redistribute your command tokens.

When you cast votes during the agenda phase, you may exhaust this card to cast 1 additional vote for each of your structures on the game board. Then, if the outcome you voted for is resolved, ready this card.

name: Psychoarchaeology
text:
When researching technology, you may use technology specialties without exhausting planets, even if they are exhausted.

During your turn, you may exhaust this card and a planet with a technology specialty to ready 1 technology you own of a matching color. That technology cannot have more prerequisites than the number of matching specialties on planets you control. This counts as using specialties for SYNERGY and other game effects.

name: Scanlink Drone Network
text:
When you activate a system, you may explore 1 planet in that system that contains 1 or more of your units.

When you would explore a planet that contains 2 or more of your structures, you may exhaust this card to draw 1 additional card; choose 1 to resolve and discard the rest.

name: Plasma Scoring
text: When your units make a roll for a unit ability, you may choose 1 of those units to roll 1 additional die.

name: Dacxive Animators
text: When your infantry on a planet is destroyed or after you win a ground combat, you may place 1 infantry on that planet. This effect can be resolved only once per planet in an action.

name: Hyper Metabolism
text:
At the end of your turn, you may exhaust this card to gain 1 command token.

During the action phase, this card cannot be readied by any game effect unless you spend 2 influence.

name: Gravity Drive
text: After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card to apply +1 to the move value of 1 of your ships during this tactical action. Then, after movement, if any ship moved through or out of a system containing a gravity rift, a supernova, or a wormhole, you may spend 1 resource to ready this card.

name: Fleet Logistics
text:
During each of your turns of the action phase, you may perform 2 actions instead of 1.

ACTION: Exhaust this card and choose 1 of your ships in a system that does not contain your command token; spend resources equal to half that ship's cost (rounded up). Then, move that ship to an adjacent system that contains your ships.

name: Graviton Laser System
text:
You may exhaust this card before your units use SPACE CANNON to apply +1 to the result of each die roll; hits produced by those units must be assigned to non-fighter ships, if able.

Your units with PLANETARY SHIELD gain SUSTAIN DAMAGE and may use it to cancel hits produced against your units in the same system.

name: Magen Defense Grid
text:
Your war suns cannot be damaged or destroyed by your opponent's action cards.

Your units apply +1 to their space cannon rolls against units within the same system.

At the start of ground combat, for each of your units with PLANETARY SHIELD on the planet, produce 1 hit and assign it to 1 of your opponent's ground forces.

name: Duranium Armor
text: At the end of a combat round, you may spend 1 resource to repair 1 of your units participating in the combat. You do not need to spend a resource if that unit did not use SUSTAIN DAMAGE during this combat round.

name: Transit Diodes
text:
You may exhaust this card at the start of your turn; remove up to 4 of your ground forces from the game board and place them on 1 or more planets you control.

You may exhaust this card before you roll dice during a round of ground combat; remove all of your ground forces in that combat and place them on 1 planet you control that contains a structure.

name: Assault Cannon
text:
At the start of a space combat in a system that contains 3 or more of your non-fighter ships, your opponent must destroy 1 of their non-fighter ships.

At the start of a space combat round, you may exhaust this card to choose a non-fighter non-ground force unit type in the active system. During this combat round, your units of that type roll twice.

name: Sling Relay
text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to produce 1 ship in any system that contains 1 of your space docks. You may instead place that ship in an adjacent system that contains 1 or more of your units or planets you control; if you do, the cost of that ship is doubled.

name: Antimass Deflectors
text:
Your ships can move into and through asteroid fields.

When other players’ units use SPACE CANNON against your units, apply -1 to the result of each die roll.

If you announce a retreat, your opponent applies -2 to the result of each of their combat rolls during this combat round.

name: Bioplasmosis
text:
When you produce units in a system that is connected to your home system by a path of adjacent systems containing your units, reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1.

ACTION: Exhaust this card to remove any number of ground forces or structures from planets you control and place them on 1 or more planets you control in the same or adjacent systems.

name: Production Biomes
text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to gain 3 trade goods and choose 1 other player; that player gains 2 trade goods.

name: Yin Spinner
text:
After you produce units, place up to 2 infantry from your reinforcements on any planet you control or in any space area that contains 1 or more of your ships.

When you spend resources to research a technology, you may spend commodities as if they were resources.

name: Instinct Training
text: You may exhaust this card when another player plays an action card; cancel that action card.

name: L4 Disruptors
text: After you spend 2 or more trade goods, gain 1 trade good.

name: Impulse Core
text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to choose a system. Destroy up to 3 of your cruisers or destroyers in that system. For each ship destroyed, produce 1 hit against another player's units in 1 adjacent system. That player must assign these hits to non-fighter ships, if able. Then, you may produce a number of infantry in your home system, up to the number of your ships destroyed.

name: Inheritance Systems
text:
When you research a technology, you may spend 2 resources and exhaust this card to ignore all of that technology's prerequisites.

After an invasion in which you gained control of a planet that contains 1 or more structures of another player, you may exhaust this card to gain 1 non-faction technology owned by that player.

name: E-Res Siphons
text: After another player activates a system that contains 1 or more of your ships, gain 3 trade goods.

name: Spatial Conduit Cylinders
text: You may exhaust this card after a player activates a system; treat each of your unit as if they were adjacent to systems that contain your structures during this tactical action.

name: Lazax Gate Folding
text:
After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card; you treat the Mecatol Rex system as if it is adjacent to each system that contains your structures.

ACTION: Exhaust this card to place and commit 2 infantry to Mecatol Rex, place 1 more if you don't have custodians token; resolve an invasion on that planet.

name: Valkyrie Particle Weave
text:
After you committed at least 1 ground force to a planet, you may commit 1 ground force from each planet in the active system and each planet in adjacent systems that do not contain 1 of your command tokens.

After making combat rolls during a round of ground combat, if your opponent produced 1 or more hits, you produce 1 additional hit.

name: Magmus Reactor
text:
Your ships can move into supernovas. Each supernova that contains 1 or more of your units gains the PRODUCTION 5 ability as if it were 1 of your units.

After you place 1 or more units in a system that contains or is adjacent to a supernova, you may gain 1 commodity.

name: Dimensional Splicer
text: At the start of a space combat in a system containing a wormhole and 1 or more of your ships, you may produce hits equal to half the number of wormholes in that system (rounded up). Assign these hits to your opponent's ships in that system.

name: Hegemonic Trade Policy
text: When 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION, you may swap the influence and resource values of 1 planet you control during this use of PRODUCTION.

name: Aerie Hololattice
text:
Other players cannot move ships through systems that contain your structures, unless you allow it.

Each planet that contains 1 or more of your structures gains the PRODUCTION 1 ability as if it were a unit.

After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card; during this tactical action, your units can move out of systems that contain both your structures and your command tokens.
